channel.title: Daily Feed (Last 48 Hours, Updated 9 AM BD)
channel.description: Daily Feed (Last 48 Hours, Updated 9 AM BD) generated by script

item.title: Where Is Iran’s Enriched Uranium?
item.description: The U.N. nuclear watchdog suspects that roughly half of Tehran’s stockpile is still at its Isfahan facility.

item.title: ‘Resign Immediately’: Democrats Grill Hegseth on Iran War
item.description: Congressional Democrats fought with the U.S. defense secretary over the war and military spending in a rare public hearing.

item.title: India Rethinks Energy Security Amid War
item.description: As New Delhi feels the crunch, diplomacy in Abu Dhabi hints at expanding green cooperation.

item.title: Europe’s unpopular leaders are paralysing the EU
item.description: Weak governments cannot conjure the support to make crucial deals

item.title: Where Is Pakistan Again?
item.description: Why the World Bank’s quiet decision to move the South Asian country to its Middle East column is more than just a bureaucratic footnote.

item.title: SpaceX, OpenAI and Anthropic are already public companies
item.description: Investors have countless ways to bet on their success

item.title: How Kevin Warsh could save the Federal Reserve
item.description: There is much to like about the new Fed chair—if his backbone holds

item.title: Trump’s War Is Damaging U.S. Arms Exports to Allies
item.description: Deals are being canceled as stockpiles run short.

item.title: Can South Africa’s Apartheid-Era Negotiator Chart a Smooth Course in the U.S.?
item.description: Pretoria stakes high hopes on its controversial pick for ambassador to Washington.

item.title: The Global Economic Impact from the Iran Conflict
item.description: Gita Gopinath on how countries can make themselves more resilient.

item.title: Swashbuckling oil-services firms are preparing for a boom
item.description: Post-war reconstruction and efforts to diversify production will present big opportunities

item.title: Could China help make Africa a factory for the world?
item.description: A spurt of Chinese investment suggests the chances are improving

item.title: A glimpse into cyber-security’s AI-driven future
item.description: A hacking conference reveals how machines will defend us

item.title: Genome editing can be risky. Meet the epigenome editors
item.description: The technology could tackle diseases such as atherosclerosis and hepatitis B

item.title: A radical idea for governing California
item.description: Democrats could try picking a young candidate who has a solid record of running a big city

item.title: Beware of Government by AI
item.description: As countries like the United Arab Emirates increasingly delegate decision-making to algorithms, often with little transparency, the risks are no longer hypothetical. Evidence shows these systems can cause widespread harm, erode democratic accountability, and concentrate power in the hands of tech titans.

item.title: A political merger kicks off Israel’s election season
item.description: Binyamin Netanyahu still has one big advantage over his opponents

item.title: The Kissinger Tapes and China-US Rapprochement
item.description: Insights from Tom Wells.

item.title: Between IMF Conditions and Rising Prices, Is Sri Lanka Heading Toward Stagflation?
item.description: If inflation is driven primarily by rising costs rather than demand, this could undermine Sri Lanka’s fragile economic recovery.

item.title: The United States Is Losing the Race for Central Asia’s Critical Minerals
item.description: Without a sustained and coordinated push, the United States risks being locked out of one of the most consequential supply chains shaping the future of global power.

item.title: Bigger Defense Budgets Are Not Enough to Secure Europe
item.description: Contrary to popular belief, the main obstacle to Europe’s strategic autonomy is not a lack of funding but its failure to develop critical technologies. Overcoming this constraint will require a fundamentally different approach to investment that better aligns public and private capital.

item.title: Swiss Karimova Corruption Case Discontinued
item.description: Karimova – already serving a 13-year sentence in Uzbekistan – wasn’t allowed to attend the trial in Switzerland and as such, the judges dismissed the case on procedural grounds.

item.title: India-US Relations Hit Another Bump
item.description: Trump’s reposting of a podcaster’s description of India as a “hellhole” is the latest of several insults from Washington.

item.title: Thailand Walked Into Its LNG Trap With Its Eyes Open – Long Before Hormuz
item.description: Bangkok’s gas bet was always going to hurt consumers. A distant conflict has merely made that reckoning impossible to defer.

item.title: The Bangladesh-US Trade Deal Is a Litmus Test for Dhaka’s Strategic Autonomy
item.description: The ART is framed as a market-access deal, but its deeper clauses are geopolitical. For Bangladesh, that represents a challenge to its traditional balancing strategy.

item.title: Preventing Online Child Abuse Should Be Easier
item.description: The international response to online child abuse continues to fall short. The problem is not that we lack known solutions or sufficient evidence to understand the challenge; it is that we have failed to apply existing tools fully, consistently, and in a globally coordinated fashion.

item.title: Former Kyrgyz Security Chief Tashiev Confirms Criminal Case Against Him
item.description: In his statement, Tashiev urges people not to protest and "act only within the law."

item.title: Middle East Dispatch: Beyond the wars
item.description: Josie Delap, our Middle East editor, reflects on the other facets of locations synonymous with strife

item.title: China’s Sanctions Hit Europe’s Emerging Drone Doctrine
item.description: Several European countries have been quietly developing a new drone doctrine built on both Ukraine’s battlefield experience and Taiwan’s high-tech components. China is trying to sever that link.

item.title: Can Central Asia Finally Coordinate on Climate and Water?
item.description: Central Asia is not yet a fully coordinated regional actor on climate and water, but it is starting to behave like one in some areas.

item.title: War Is Killing Ukraine’s Oligarchy
item.description: The fight against Russia is accomplishing what years of struggle against corruption couldn’t.

item.title: Hedging Is the New Normal
item.description: For the foreseeable future, successful statecraft will depend on hedging.

item.title: The EU Is the New Go-To Middle Power
item.description: In a world of disorder, the bloc’s boring stability is suddenly attractive.

item.title: Living Without an AI Kill Switch
item.description: With the arrival of Anthropic’s scarily powerful Mythos model, the tech titans that have long argued against any kind of AI regulation are now clamoring for it. Governments should take immediate steps to coordinate effectively and enable rapid information sharing, while also compelling developers to meet basic safety standards.

item.title: The Taiwan Evacuation Trap
item.description: Washington has no good options for evacuating Americans in a crisis.

item.title: German Foreign Policy Has Finally Grown Up
item.description: Friedrich Merz is, slowly but surely, putting his country on a new international footing.

item.title: Why the Pakistani President’s Visit to China Amid the West Asia Crisis Is Important
item.description: Zardari’s visit focused on CPEC’s second phase, exploring new avenues for bilateral trade and investment, and advancing regional connectivity.

item.title: Why Indonesia Floated a Malacca Toll
item.description: An apparently off-the-cuff observation sparked consternation across the region.

item.title: The Real Threat to Taiwan
item.description: America Is preparing for the wrong kind of crisis

item.title: How the War Saved the Iranian Regime
item.description: The unintended consequences of the U.S.-Israeli assault.

item.title: The Transatlantic MAGA Fantasy
item.description: How Trump misread Europe.

item.title: Southeast Asia Hedging Bets and Alignment Webs Beyond the US-China Lens
item.description: Beyond U.S.-China competition or rules-based order anxiety, states are pursuing their own North Stars in geopolitics and geoeconomics.

item.title: Interpreting the Future of Vietnam-China Relations Through the 2026 Joint Statement
item.description: The recent visit to China of Vietnam's top leader To Lam could well mark a turning point in the sometimes fraught bilateral relationship.

item.title: Why DeepSeek’s sequel failed to impress
item.description: The AI lab faces stiffening competition and a meddling state

item.title: To fight Russia, Europe needs Ukraine
item.description: It is risky to let Ukraine into the EU. It’s more dangerous to keep it out

item.title: Voices Erased: Meet Bengal’s Deleted Voters
item.description: A whopping 2.7 million voters have been deleted from the electoral rolls in West Bengal after the controversial SIR exercise. Here are some of their stories.

item.title: Emmanuel Macron armours France against an Orban-style takeover
item.description: What if the National Rally wins, in one of Europe’s most centralised states?
